title: Analysis capability registry entry
description: One self-describing entry per registered analysis. The popstats / atlas server loads the whole registry (an array of these) at startup; for each incoming request it looks up the matching analysis_type and decides whether to run live, hit a cache, or read from precompute, using `execution_modes` + `default_policy` modulated by the cost-based classifier (see cost-based_usage_data-management-policy-prompt.md).

field_ownership.rule: An engine-side manifest (shipped by the toolkit repo, e.g. unified-ancestry/registries/analysis_capability.json) ONLY fills engine_side fields — those describe what the binary is technically capable of. The downstream server's deployment registry MERGES the engine manifest with deployment_side fields (cost_class, default_policy, ...) — those describe what THIS deployment will actually do with the analysis. Same schema, two readers.

analysis_type.description: Stable id of the analysis (e.g. 'fst_profile', 'pi_NS', 'iHS_window'). Used as the discriminator when routing requests.

module.description: Owning module / engine binary (e.g. 'popstats', 'codon_stats', 'fdM'). Multiple analyses can belong to one module.

provides.description: Result-schema version tags this analysis emits, e.g. ['fst_profile_v1', 'fst_profile_v2']. Clients select by version.

requires.description: Logical inputs this analysis needs. Free-form tokens; suggested vocabulary: 'beagle_gl', 'sample_list', 'groups', 'karyotype', 'interval', 'candidates_bed', 'windows_tsv', 'cache', 'genome_fa', 'gff', etc. Used by the server for pre-flight validation and for matching against what the request supplies.

supported_execution_modes.description: ENGINE-SIDE field. Which storage tiers the engine is even *technically* capable of feeding. True = the binary's output is shaped right for the mode; false = the engine actively does not support it. The deployment server further narrows this with `allowed_policies`.

live.description: Engine returns small enough output fast enough to be served live (no caching needed).

memory_cache.description: Output is small and safe to keep in RAM.

disk_cache.description: Output is deterministic given inputs — safe to memoize on disk by hash.

precompute.description: Engine can be run offline and its output read back later.

committed.description: Output is a stable artifact suitable for git / release packaging.

default_policy.description: DEPLOYMENT-SIDE field. Policy applied by the server when the cost-based classifier has no opinion. Must be a mode set to true in `supported_execution_modes`. Engine repos leave this absent; deployment repos fill it.

allowed_policies.description: DEPLOYMENT-SIDE field. Explicit allow-list (a subset of `supported_execution_modes`-true). Useful when the engine can technically do a mode but the deployment forbids it (e.g. 'no live for >1Mb intervals'). Engine repos leave this absent.

cost_class.description: DEPLOYMENT-SIDE field. Default cost class for typical-shape requests in THIS deployment. Engine repos leave it absent; the deployment fills it (the same engine can be 'cheap' for a tiny cohort and 'expensive' for a 226-sample one).

reuse_scope.description: DEPLOYMENT-SIDE field. Free-form tag describing the natural unit of reuse for THIS deployment. Suggested values: 'candidate' / 'chromosome' / 'cohort' / 'genome' / 'session' / 'ephemeral'. Engine repos leave it absent.

result_schema.description: Output JSON schema id (e.g. 'fst_result_v1'). Should match one of the entries in `provides` and correspond to a schema file the server can validate against.

entrypoint.description: How the server invokes this analysis. For engine-binary entrypoints this is the binary name (e.g. 'popstats_fst'); for dispatcher-routed analyses it's the statistic discriminator (e.g. 'outlier_scan' — handed to popstats_dispatch via the activator's `.statistic` field).

engine_binding_ref.description: Optional. Path or $id of the activator schema whose `engine_binding` block describes the CLI flag mapping. Lets the dispatcher build argv off the schema instead of hardcoding.

deprecated.description: When true, the server should warn callers that the analysis is being phased out.

notes.description: Free-form notes for humans reading the registry.

consistency_rules.default_in_modes: default_policy (deployment side) MUST correspond to a `supported_execution_modes` entry set to true.
consistency_rules.allowed_in_modes: Every value in allowed_policies (deployment side) MUST correspond to a `supported_execution_modes` entry set to true.
consistency_rules.provides_has_schema: result_schema SHOULD appear in `provides` (canonical version) OR be a strict superset version.

engine_side_example.comment: What unified-ancestry (or any toolkit repo) ships in its registries/analysis_capability.json. NO policy fields.

deployment_side_example.comment: What a downstream server adds on top, per-deployment. Policy fields only.
